_comment: Labeled ground truth for the evaluation harness (python -m app.eval).
Bands are deliberately anchored to the TRIAGE THRESHOLDS in config.py, not to exact
scores: triage_medium_threshold=40 (below this an alert auto-closes and no human ever
sees it) and triage_high_threshold=80 (at or above this it is flagged/escalated).
A band violation therefore means a real OPERATIONAL difference - an attack that would
be silently auto-closed, or benign noise that would page an analyst - rather than
harmless score jitter from the model's temperature.
Keep bands wide enough to tolerate that jitter and narrow enough to catch regressions.

cases.why: Routine scheduled backup job. MUST auto-close (<40) - paging on cron is pure noise.

Successful key-based login by sysadmin from a known internal admin workstation. MUST auto-close. (Was the flagship false positive: the agent inherited critical(95) from its OWN unverified prior verdict. Fixed by discounting unverified memories and forcing a tool call before submit - now scores ~15.)
Normal apt package update. MUST auto-close.
Recon only, no compromise. Should be visible but NOT escalated as critical.
Brute-force attempts, not yet successful. Should be queued for a human.
Successful login after repeated failures = actual compromise. Should escalate.
Active SQLi with UNION SELECT against a live app.
Web shell on disk = remote code execution foothold. Should escalate.
www-data escalating to root is a hard compromise signal.
4.5 GiB outbound to an untrusted host vs a 12 MiB/day baseline.
Mass file rename + ransom note. The single most severe scenario - must escalate.

Repeated SSH auth failures from 185.220.101.1. BAND WIDENED 89->100 ON PURPOSE: an ANALYST-CONFIRMED memory (id 25, human_action=confirm, score 95) records a SUCCESSFUL login from this same IP, so escalating is correct here - the agent is obeying the 'analyst verdicts are authoritative' rule, not over-reacting. If that confirmed record is ever removed, tighten this back to 40-89.
Auth SUCCESS from an IP with prior failures - the memory layer should raise this.

EICAR is a harmless standard AV TEST file that does nothing. The correct expert verdict is LOW. Band was 55-100 and has been tightened DOWNWARD, honouring the prediction recorded in it beforehand: it used to score 90+ by trusting VirusTotal's 'malicious' count blindly, and under stage-anchored scoring the agent now reasons that the file cannot cause harm. Escalating on EICAR is a false positive, so this case now tests exactly that.
